{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYEOF'\nimport re\n\nwith open('.planning/PROJECT.md', 'r') as f:\n    content = f.read()\n\nold = \"\"\"## Current Milestone: v2.0 Wave-Based Hierarchical Semantic Analysis\n\n**Goal:** Revamp the UKB pipeline from a flat batch DAG into a multi-agent system operating in hierarchical waves. Wave 1 identifies project building blocks with comprehensive summaries. Wave 2 creates sub-nodes for each component with detailed insights (md, puml/png). Wave 3+ adds sub-sub-nodes with increasing detail. Each hierarchy level is self-sufficient and useful on its own.\n\n**Foundation available:** Hierarchy fields in schema (v1.0 Phase 4), 30 entities already organized L0→L3 (migration script), VKB blue gradient (darker at stem, lighter at leaves).\n\n**Key changes from flat DAG:**\n- Pipeline runs in waves (L0→L1→L2→L3), not a single flat pass\n- Each wave spawns agents focused on one hierarchy level\n- Observations are rich and numerous (not one-liner stubs)\n- Every entity gets detailed insight documents (markdown + PlantUML)\n- Sub-nodes reflect actual architecture, not just \"Pipeline\" / \"Insights\\\"\"\"\"\n\nnew = \"\"\"## Current Milestone: v2.1 Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration\n\n**Goal:** Re-integrate the full multi-agent pipeline (semantic analysis, KG operators, content validation, QA, ontology classification, trace reporting) into the wave-based architecture. v2.0 built the hierarchical wave structure but dropped the rich agent pipeline — this milestone restores it.\n\n**Foundation available:** Working wave-controller (3 waves + insight finalization), 691 entities in KG, component manifest, ontology classification agent (partially restored), persistence agent, insight generation agent.\n\n**Key problems to fix:**\n- Wave agents do lightweight LLM calls — need deep semantic analysis integration\n- KG operators (conv, aggr, embed, dedup, pred, merge) completely skipped\n- Content validation disabled in wave persistence\n- No QA step in wave pipeline\n- Trace modal broken (no LLM counts, no timing, no model info)\n- Ontology classification was auto-assigning hierarchy level as class (partially fixed)\n- Hallucination risk from unbounded L3 suggestions (basic guards added)\"\"\"\n\ncontent = content.replace(old, new)\n\n# Update last updated\ncontent = re.sub(r'\\*Last updated:.*\\*', '*Last updated: 2026-03-07 after v2.1 milestone start*', content)\n\nwith open('.planning/PROJECT.md', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(content)\n\nprint(\"PROJECT.md updated\")\nPYEOF",
  "description": "Update PROJECT.md for v2.1 milestone"
}
```

percent: 0\n---\n\n# Project State\n\n## Project Reference\n\nSee: .planning/PROJECT.md (updated 2026-03-07)\n\n**Core value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge\n**Current focus:** Defining requirements for v2.1 — full agent pipeline restoration\n\n## Current Position\n\nPhase: Not started (defining requirements)\nPlan: —\nStatus: Defining requirements\nLast activity: 2026-03-07 — Milestone v2.1 started\n\nProgress: [░░░░░░░░░░] 0%\n\n## Accumulated Context\n\n### Decisions\n\n- [v2.0]: Wave-based hierarchical architecture is correct (keep it)\n- [v2.0]: Hierarchy: L0 Project → L1 Component → L2 SubComponent → L3 Detail\n- [v2.1]: Wave pipeline dropped rich agent pipeline — must restore ALL agents\n- [v2.1]: Ontology classification partially fixed (this session) — needs completion\n- [v2.1]: Basic hallucination guards added (L3 caps, code evidence filter)\n- [v2.1]: Trace modal broken — wave controller doesn't report LLM metrics\n\n### Critical Pitfalls\n\n- **processEntity() hierarchy mapping**: Must extend — fields silently dropped without it\n- **Dedup collapse**: mergeEntities() overwrites parentId with undefined — needs null-coalesce fix\n- **LevelDB/JSON sync**: Must use GraphDatabaseAdapter, not GraphDatabaseService\n- **Docker rebuild**: Pipeline changes require submodule build + Docker rebuild\n- **Submodule build**: npm run build in submodule BEFORE Docker rebuild (recurring issue)\n- **Wave agents use own LLM calls**: Not routed through SemanticAnalyzer — no trace data collected\n- **KG operators never called**: Wave persistence skips conv/aggr/embed/dedup/pred/merge entirely\n\n### Blockers/Concerns\n\nNone.\n\n## Session Continuity\n\nLast session: 2026-03-07T09:00:00Z\nStopped at: Defining requirements for v2.1\nResume with: Complete requirements → create roadmap\n\"\"\"\n\nwith open('.planning/STATE.md', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(state)\n\nprint(\"STATE.md updated\")\nPYEOF",
  "description": "Update STATE.md for v2.1 milestone"
}

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -t /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -5",
  "description": "List most recent LSL files"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0900-1000_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0800-0900_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1900-2000_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1800-1900_c197ef.md
```


---

---

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -t /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -5",
  "description": "List most recent LSL files"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0900-1000_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0800-0900_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1900-2000_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1800-1900_c197ef.md
```


---

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Read
**Input:** ```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md",
  "limit": 200
}
```

**Result:** ❌ Error

---

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -t /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -5",
  "description": "List most recent LSL files"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0900-1000_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0800-0900_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1900-2000_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1800-1900_c197ef.md
```


---

### Read - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Read
**Input:** ```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_0900-1000_c197ef.md",
  "limit": 200
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
     1→# WORK SESSION (0900-1000)
     2→
     3→**Generated:** 2026-03-07T08:09:32.243Z
     4→**Work Period:** 0900-1000
     5→**Focus:** Live session logging
     6→**Duration:** ~60 minutes
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Session Overview
    11→
    12→This session captures real-time tool interactions and exchanges.
    13→
    14→---
    15→
    16→## Key Activities
    17→
    18→<a name="ps_1772870405323"></a>
    19→## Prompt Set (ps_1772870405323)
    20→
    21→**Time:** 2026-03
...[truncated]
```

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -t /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -5",
  "description": "List most recent LSL files"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```

2026-03-07_0900-1000_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0800-0900_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1900-2000_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1800-1900_c197ef.md
```

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "wc -l /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md",
  "description": "Check size of most recent LSL file"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
2862 /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md
```

### Read - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Read
**Input:** ```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_0900-1000_c197ef.md",
  "limit": 200
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success

     3→**Generated:** 2026-03-07T08:09:32.243Z

     6→**Duration:** ~60 minutes

    12→This session captures real-time tool interactions and exchanges.
    13→

    15→
    16→## Key Activities
    17→
    18→<a name="ps_1772870405323"></a>

analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated (fix partial restore)\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### Content <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [ ] **QUAL-01**: Content validation enforced in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **QUAL-02**: QA agent validates every agent output (not a single pipeline stage)\n- [ ] **QUAL-03**: Coordinator receives QA feedback and can reject/retry agent outputs\n- [ ] **QUAL-04**: Hallucination guards completed (L3 caps, code evidence filter, prompt hardening)\n\n### Observability\n\n- [ ] **OBSV-01**: Trace modal displays LLM call counts per wave and agent\n- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Trace modal displays timing breakdown per agent and wave\n- [ ] **OBSV-03**: Trace modal displays model info (which LLM, provider)\n- [ ] **OBSV-04**: Trace modal shows data flow — what went in/out of each agent to diagnose information loss\n\n## Future Requirements\n\n### VKB Navigation (deferred to v2.2)\n\n- **VKBN-01**: Tree navigation with hierarchy drill-down\n- **VKBN-02**: Breadcrumb navigation showing hierarchy path\n- **VKBN-03**: Entity   "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated (fix partial restore)\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### Content <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [ ] **QUAL-06**: Content validation enforced in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **QUAL-07**: QA agent validates every agent output (not a single pipeline stage)\n- [ ] **QUAL-08**: Coordinator receives QA feedback and can reject/retry agent outputs\n- [ ] **QUAL-09**: Hallucination guards completed (L3 caps, code evidence filter, prompt hardening)\n\n### Observability\n\n- [ ] **OBSV-01**: Trace modal displays LLM call counts per wave and agent\n- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Trace modal displays timing breakdown per agent and wave\n- [ ] **OBSV-03**: Trace modal displays model info (which LLM, provider)\n- [ ] **OBSV-04**: Trace modal shows data flow — what went in/out of each agent to diagnose information loss\n\n## Future Requirements\n\n### VKB Navigation (deferred to v2.2)\n\n- **VKBN-01**: Tree navigation with hierarchy drill-down\n- **VKBN-02**: Breadcrumb navigation showing hierarchy path\n- **VKBN-03**: Entity detail view with observations and insight documents\n\n### Deferred from v1.0/v2.0\n\n- **QUAL-04**: VKB entity detail panel shows \"Detailed Insight\" link to the insight document\n- **VKB-01**: Collapsible tree sidebar showing full hierarchy\n- **VKB-02**: Clicking a tree node filters the D3 graph to that subtree\n- **VKB-03**: Breadcrumb navigation computed from parent chain\n- **VKB-04**: Blue gradient visualization persists\n- **SIG-01**: Significance scoring for entities\n- **SIG-02**: <USER_ID_REDACTED> ranking for observations\n\n## Out of Scope\n\n| Feature | Reason |\n|---------|--------|\n| VKB tree navigation | Deferred to v2.2 — focus v2.1 on pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> |\n| MCP server interface changes | `ukb full` invocation must stay the same |\n| Agent framework rewrite | Extend existing coordinator, don't rewrite |\n| Real-time pipeline monitoring | v2+ concern, trace modal is sufficient for now |\n| Significance scoring | v1.0 deferral, revisit after pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> restored |\n\n## Traceability\n\nWhich phases cover which requirements. Updated during roadmap creation.\n\n### v2.0 (Shipped)\n\n| Requirement | Phase | Status |\n|-------------|-------|--------|\n| WAVE-01 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-02 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-03 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-04 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-05 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-06 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| QUAL-01 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-02 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-03 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-05 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| HIER-01 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-02 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-03 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-04 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n\n### v2.1\n\n| Requirement | Phase | Status |\n|-------------|-------|--------|\n| AGNT-01 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-02 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-03 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-04 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-05 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-01 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-02 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-03 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-04 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-05 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-06 | — | Pending |\n| QUAL-06 | — | Pending |\n| QUAL-07 | — | Pending |\n| QUAL-08 | — | Pending |\n| QUAL-09 | — | Pending |\n| OBSV-01 | — | Pending |\n| OBSV-02 | — | Pending |\n| OBSV-03 | — | Pending |\n| OBSV-04 | — | Pending |\n\n**Coverage:**\n- v2.1 requirements: 19 total\n- Mapped to phases: 0\n- Unmapped: 19\n\n---\n*Requirements defined: 2026-03-04*\n*Last updated: 2026-03-07 after v2.1 requirements definition*\n"

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -t /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -5",
  "description": "List most recent LSL files"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0900-1000_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_0800-0900_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1900-2000_c197ef.md
2026-03-06_1800-1900_c197ef.md
```


---

### Read - 2026-03-07 09:13:37 UTC [10:13:37 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

}
```

not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### Content <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [ ] **QUAL-06**: Content validation enforced in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **QUAL-07**: QA agent validates every agent output (continuous gate, not single pipeline stage)\n- [ ] **QUAL-08**: Coordinator receives QA feedback and can reject/retry agent outputs\n- [ ] **QUAL-09**: Hallucination guards completed (L3 caps, code evidence filter, prompt hardening)\n\n### Observability\n\n- [ ] **OBSV-01**: Trace modal displays LLM call counts per wave and agent\n- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Trace modal displays timing breakdown per agent and wave\n- [ ] **OBSV-03**: Trace modal displays model info (which LLM, provider)\n- [ ] **OBSV-04**: Trace modal shows data flow — what went in/out of each agent to diagnose information loss\n\n## Future Requirements\n\n### VKB Navigation (v2.2)\n\n- **VKBN-01**: Tree navigation with hierarchy drill-down\n- **VKBN-02**: Breadcrumb navigation showing hierarchy path\n- **VKBN-03**: Entity detail view with observations and insight documents\n- **QUAL-04**: VKB entity detail panel shows \"Detailed Insight\" link to the insight document\n- **VKB-01**: Collapsible tree sidebar showing full hierarchy\n- **VKB-02**: Clicking a tree node filters the D3 graph to that subtree\n- **VKB-03**: Breadcrumb navigation computed from parent chain\n- **VKB-04**: Blue gradient visualization persists\n\n### Scoring (future)\n\n- **SIG-01**: Significance scoring for entities\n- **SIG-02**: <USER_ID_REDACTED> ranking for observations\n\n## Out of Scope\n\n| Feature | Reason |\n|---------|--------|\n| VKB tree navigation | v2.2 scope — v2.1 focuses on pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> |\n| MCP server interface changes | `ukb full` invocation must stay the same |\n| Agent framework rewrite | Extend existing coordinator, don't rewrite |\n| Real-time pipeline monitoring | Trace modal is sufficient for current needs |\n| Significance scoring | Revisit after pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> restored |\n\n## Traceability\n\nWhich phases cover which requirements.\n\n### v2.0 (Shipped)\n\n| Requirement | Phase | Status |\n|-------------|-------|--------|\n| WAVE-01 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-02 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-03 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-04 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-05 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-06 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n|   "new_string": "  whitelist:\n  - '**/README.md'\n  - '**/CHANGELOG.md'\n  - '**/CLAUDE.md'\n  - '**/MEMORY.md'\n  - '**/INSTALL*.md'\n  - CLAUDE.md\n  # GSD planning files use uppercase by convention\n  # Need both patterns: *.md for direct files, **/*.md for subdirectories\n  - '**/.planning/*.md'\n  - '**/.planning/**/*.md'"

  "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator   "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n###   "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### Content <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [ ] **QUAL-06**: Content validation enforced in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **QUAL-07**: QA agent validates every agent output (continuous gate, not single pipeline stage)\n- [ ] **QUAL-08**: Coordinator receives QA feedback and can reject/retry agent outputs\n- [ ] **QUAL-09**: Hallucination guards completed (L3 caps, code evidence filter, prompt hardening)\n\n### Observability\n\n- [ ] **OBSV-01**: Trace modal displays LLM call counts per wave and agent\n- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Trace modal displays timing breakdown per agent and wave\n- [ ] **OBSV-03**: Trace modal displays model info (which LLM, provider)\n- [ ] **OBSV-04**: Trace modal shows data flow — what went in/out of each agent to diagnose information loss\n\n## Future Requirements\n\n### VKB Navigation (v2.2)\n\n- **VKBN-01**: Tree navigation with hierarchy drill-down\n- **VKBN-02**: Breadcrumb navigation showing hierarchy path\n- **VKBN-03**: Entity detail view hierarchy\n- **VKB-02**: Clicking a tree node filters the D3 graph to that subtree\n- **VKB-03**: Breadcrumb navigation computed from parent chain\n- **VKB-04**: Blue gradient visualization   "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### hardening)\n\n### Observability\n\n- [ ] **OBSV-01**: Trace modal displays LLM call counts per wave and agent\n- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Trace modal displays timing breakdown per agent and wave\n- [ ]   "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md",
  "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### Content <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [ ] **QUAL-06**: Content validation enforced in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **QUAL-07**: QA agent validates every agent output (continuous gate, not single pipeline stage)\n- [ ] **QUAL-08**: Coordinator receives QA feedback and can reject/retry agent outputs\n- [ ] **QUAL-09**: Hallucination guards completed (L3 caps, code evidence filter, prompt hardening)\n\n### Observability\n\n- [ ] **OBSV-01**: Trace modal displays LLM call counts per wave and agent\n- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Trace modal displays timing breakdown per agent and wave\n- [ ] **OBSV-03**: Trace modal displays model info (which LLM, provider)\n- [ ] **OBSV-04**: Trace modal shows data flow — what went in/out of each agent to diagnose information loss\n\n## Future Requirements\n\n### VKB Navigation (v2.2)\n\n- **VKBN-01**: Tree navigation with hierarchy drill-down\n- **VKBN-02**: Breadcrumb navigation showing hierarchy path\n- **VKBN-03**: Entity detail view with observations and insight documents\n- **QUAL-04**: VKB entity detail panel shows \"Detailed Insight\" link to the insight document\n- **VKB-01**: Collapsible tree sidebar showing full hierarchy\n- **VKB-02**: Clicking a tree node filters the D3 graph to that subtree\n- **VKB-03**: Breadcrumb navigation computed from parent chain\n- **VKB-04**: Blue gradient visualization   "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### Content <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [ ] **QUAL-06**: Content validation enforced in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **QUAL-07**: QA agent validates every agent output (continuous gate, not single pipeline stage)\n- [ ] **QUAL-08**: Coordinator receives QA feedback and can reject/retry agent outputs\n- [ ] **QUAL-09**: Hallucination guards completed (L3 caps, code evidence filter, prompt hardening)\n\n### Observability\n\n- [ ] **OBSV-01**: Trace modal displays LLM call counts per wave and agent\n- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Trace modal displays timing breakdown per agent and wave\n- [ ] **OBSV-03**: Trace modal displays model info (which LLM, provider)\n- [ ] **OBSV-04**: Trace modal shows data flow — what went in/out of each agent to diagnose information loss\n\n## Future Requirements\n\n### VKB Navigation (v2.2)\n\n- **VKBN-01**: Tree navigation with hierarchy drill-down\n- **VKBN-02**: Breadcrumb navigation showing hierarchy path\n- **VKBN-03**: Entity detail view with observations and insight documents\n- **QUAL-04**: VKB entity detail panel shows \"Detailed Insight\" link to the insight document\n- **VKB-01**: Collapsible tree sidebar showing full hierarchy\n- **VKB-02**: Clicking a tree node filters the D3 graph to that subtree\n- **VKB-03**: Breadcrumb navigation computed from parent chain\n- **VKB-04**: Blue gradient visualization persists\n\n### Scoring (future)\n\n- **SIG-01**: Significance scoring for entities\n- **SIG-02**: <USER_ID_REDACTED> ranking for observations\n\n## Out of Scope\n\n| Feature | Reason |\n|---------|--------|\n| VKB tree navigation | v2.2 scope — v2.1 focuses on pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> |\n| MCP server interface changes | `ukb full` invocation must stay the same |\n| Agent framework rewrite | Extend existing coordinator, don't rewrite |\n| Real-time pipeline monitoring | Trace modal is sufficient for current needs |\n| Significance scoring | Revisit after pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> restored |\n\n## Traceability\n\nWhich phases cover which requirements.\n\n### v2.0 (Shipped)\n\n| Requirement | Phase | Status |\n|-------------|-------|--------|\n| WAVE-01 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-02 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-03 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-04 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-05 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-06 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| QUAL-01 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-02 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-03 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-05 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| HIER-01 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-02 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-03 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-04 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n\n### v2.1\n\n| Requirement | Phase | Status |\n|-------------|-------|--------|\n| AGNT-01 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-02 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-03 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-04 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-05 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-01 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-02 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-03 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-04 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-05 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-06 | — | Pending |\n| QUAL-06 | — | Pending |\n| QUAL-07 | — | Pending |\n| QUAL-08 | — | Pending |\n| QUAL-09 | — | Pending |\n| OBSV-01 | — | Pending |\n| OBSV-02 | — | Pending |\n| OBSV-03 | — | Pending |\n| OBSV-04 | — | Pending |\n\n**Coverage:**\n- v2.1 requirements: 19 total\n- Mapped to phases: 0\n- Unmapped: 19\n\n---\n*Last updated: 2026-03-07*\n"
}

  "new_string": "  whitelist:\n  - '**/README.md'\n  - '**/CHANGELOG.md'\n  - '**/CLAUDE.md'\n  - '**/MEMORY.md'\n  - '**/INSTALL*.md'\n  - CLAUDE.md\n  # GSD planning files use uppercase by convention\n  # Need both patterns: *.md for direct files, **/*.md for subdirectories\n  - '**/.planning/*.md'\n  - '**/.planning/**/*.md'"
}

  "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### Use git for history.'\n  severity: error\n  enabled: true\n  applies_to: content\n  tool_filter:\n  - Edit\n  - Write\n  file_pattern: \\.md$\n  suggestion: Remove dates and incremental language. Describe how the system works\n    NOW, not when it changed. Git provides version history.\n  whitelist:\n  - CHANGELOG.md\n  - RELEASE*.md\n  - '**/.planning/**/*.md'"
}
```
