  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md",
  "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### Content <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [ ] **QUAL-06**: Content validation enforced in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **QUAL-07**: QA agent validates every agent output (continuous gate, not single pipeline stage)\n- [ ] **QUAL-08**: Coordinator receives QA feedback and can reject/retry agent outputs\n- [ ] **QUAL-09**: Hallucination guards completed (L3 caps, code evidence filter, prompt hardening)\n\n### Observability\n\n- [ ] **OBSV-01**: Trace modal displays LLM call counts per wave and agent\n- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Trace modal displays timing breakdown per agent and wave\n- [ ] **OBSV-03**: Trace modal displays model info (which LLM, provider)\n- [ ] **OBSV-04**: Trace modal shows data flow — what went in/out of each agent to diagnose information loss\n\n## Future Requirements\n\n### VKB Navigation (v2.2)\n\n- **VKBN-01**: Tree navigation with hierarchy drill-down\n- **VKBN-02**: Breadcrumb navigation showing hierarchy path\n- **VKBN-03**: Entity detail view with observations and insight documents\n- **QUAL-04**: VKB entity detail panel shows \"Detailed Insight\" link to the insight document\n- **VKB-01**: Collapsible tree sidebar showing full hierarchy\n- **VKB-02**: Clicking a tree node filters the D3 graph to that subtree\n- **VKB-03**: Breadcrumb navigation computed from parent chain\n- **VKB-04**: Blue gradient visualization persists\n\n### Scoring (future)\n\n- **SIG-01**: Significance scoring for entities\n- **SIG-02**: <USER_ID_REDACTED> ranking for observations\n\n## Out of Scope\n\n| Feature | Reason |\n|---------|--------|\n| VKB tree navigation | v2.2 scope — v2.1 focuses on pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> |\n| MCP server interface changes | `ukb full` invocation must stay the same |\n| Agent framework rewrite | Extend existing coordinator, don't rewrite |\n| Real-time pipeline monitoring | Trace modal is sufficient for current needs |\n| Significance scoring | Revisit after pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> restored |\n\n## Traceability\n\nWhich phases cover which requirements.\n\n### v2.0 (Shipped)\n\n| Requirement | Phase | Status |\n|-------------|-------|--------|\n| WAVE-01 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-02 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-03 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-04 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-05 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| WAVE-06 | Phase 5 | Complete |\n| QUAL-01 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-02 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-03 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| QUAL-05 | Phase 6 | Complete |\n| HIER-01 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-02 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-03 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n| HIER-04 | Phase 7 | Complete |\n\n### v2.1\n\n| Requirement | Phase | Status |\n|-------------|-------|--------|\n| AGNT-01 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-02 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-03 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-04 | — | Pending |\n| AGNT-05 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-01 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-02 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-03 | — | Pending |\n| KGOP-04 | — | Pending   "content": "# Requirements: Coding Project Knowledge Management\n\n**Defined:** 2026-03-04\n**Core Value:** Wave-based multi-agent pipeline producing self-sufficient hierarchical knowledge at every level\n\n## v2.0 Requirements (Shipped)\n\n### Wave Orchestration\n\n- [x] **WAVE-01**: Pipeline executes analysis in hierarchical waves (L0->L1->L2->L3) instead of a flat batch pass\n- [x] **WAVE-02**: Wave 1 agent surveys the entire project and produces L0 Project node + L1 Component nodes with comprehensive summaries\n- [x] **WAVE-03**: Wave 2 agents receive L1 results and produce L2 SubComponent nodes with detailed observations per component\n- [x] **WAVE-04**: Wave 3 agents receive L2 results and produce L3 Detail nodes with specific, deep knowledge\n- [x] **WAVE-05**: Wave controller ensures wave N completes fully before wave N+1 spawns\n- [x] **WAVE-06**: Within each wave, agents run in parallel (one agent per parent node being expanded)\n\n### Entity <USER_ID_REDACTED>\n\n- [x] **QUAL-01**: Each entity produced by the pipeline has 3+ meaningful, specific observations (not one-liner stubs)\n- [x] **QUAL-02**: Each entity gets a detailed insight document (markdown with architecture context, purpose, patterns)\n- [x] **QUAL-03**: Insight documents include PlantUML diagrams for architectural entities\n- [x] **QUAL-05**: Insight documents cross-reference related entities and parent/child relationships\n\n### Hierarchy Completeness\n\n- [x] **HIER-01**: L1 components have comprehensive sub-node coverage reflecting actual architecture (not just 2-3 children)\n- [x] **HIER-02**: Sub-nodes represent real architectural aspects discovered from code analysis, not generic labels\n- [x] **HIER-03**: Component manifest is auto-extended based on wave analysis discoveries\n- [x] **HIER-04**: Each hierarchy level provides self-sufficient knowledge useful at that granularity\n\n## v2.1 Requirements\n\nRequirements for Wave Pipeline <USER_ID_REDACTED> Restoration. Each maps to roadmap phases.\n\n### Agent Integration\n\n- [ ] **AGNT-01**: Wave agent LLM calls route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep, multi-observation analysis\n- [ ] **AGNT-02**: Semantic analysis agent integrated into wave pipeline (all 3 waves)\n- [ ] **AGNT-03**: Persistence agent restored in wave persistence path\n- [ ] **AGNT-04**: Insight generation agent produces detailed insight documents per entity\n- [ ] **AGNT-05**: Ontology classification agent fully integrated into wave pipeline\n\n### KG Operations\n\n- [ ] **KGOP-01**: Conversion operator (conv) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-02**: Aggregation operator (aggr) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-03**: Embedding operator (embed) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-04**: Dedup operator enabled with fuzzy name matching\n- [ ] **KGOP-05**: Prediction operator (pred) enabled in wave persistence\n- [ ] **KGOP-06**: Merge operator enabled with null-coalesce fix for parentId\n\n### **User Request:** <objective>

### ToolSearch - 2026-03-07 09:43:50 UTC [10:43:50 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** ToolSearch
**Input:** ```json
{
  "query": "select:Bash,Glob,Read",
  "max_results": 3
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
[
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Bash"
  },
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Glob"
  },
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Read"
  }
]
```


---

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:43:50 UTC [10:43:50 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "wc -l /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md",
  "description": "Check line count of most recent LSL"
}
```

**Result:** ❌ Error

---

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:43:50 UTC [10:43:50 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "wc -l /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md",
  "description": "Check line count of most recent LSL"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
48445 /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md
```


---

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:43:50 UTC [10:43:50 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "wc -l /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md",
  "description": "Check line count of most recent LSL"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
48445 /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-07_1000-1100_c197ef.md
```

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

### Bash - 2026-03-07 09:51:27 UTC [10:51:27 CEST]

**User Request:** <objective>
Extract implementation decisions that downstream agents need — researcher and planner will use CONTEXT.md to know what to investigate and what choices are locked.

**How it works:**
1. Load prior context (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, STATE.md, prior CONTEXT.md files)
2. Scout codebase for reusable assets and patterns
3. Analyze phase — skip gray areas already decided in prior phases
4. Present remaining gray areas — user selects which to discuss
5. Deep-dive each selected area until satisfied
6. Create CONTEXT.md with decisions that guide research and planning

**Output:** `{phase_num}-CONTEXT.md` — decisions clear enough that downstream agents can act without asking the user again
</objective>

@/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/.claude/get-shit-done/templates/context.md

<context>
Phase number: 9 (required)

Context files are resolved in-workflow using `init phase-op` and roadmap/state tool calls.
</context>

<process>
1. Validate phase number (error if missing or not in roadmap)
2. Check if CONTEXT.md exists (offer update/view/skip if yes)
3. **Load prior context** — Read PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, STATE.md, and all prior CONTEXT.md files
4. **Scout codebase** — Find reusable assets, patterns, and integration points
5. **Analyze phase** — Check prior decisions, skip already-decided areas, generate remaining gray areas
6. **Present gray areas** — Multi-select: which to discuss? Annotate with prior decisions + code context
7. **Deep-dive each area** — 4 questions per area, code-informed options, Context7 for library choices
8. **Write CONTEXT.md** — Sections match areas discussed + code_context section
9. Offer next steps (research or plan)

**CRITICAL: Scope guardrail**
- Phase boundary from ROADMAP.md is FIXED
- Discussion clarifies HOW to implement, not WHETHER to add more
- If user suggests new capabilities: "That's its own phase. I'll note it for later."
- Capture deferred ideas — don't lose them, don't act on them

**Domain-aware gray areas:**
Gray areas depend on what's being built. Analyze the phase goal:
- Something users SEE → layout, density, interactions, states
- Something users CALL → responses, errors, auth, versioning
- Something users RUN → output format, flags, modes, error handling
- Something users READ → structure, tone, depth, flow
- Something being ORGANIZED → criteria, grouping, naming, exceptions

Generate 3-4 **phase-specific** gray areas, not generic categories.

**Probing depth:**
- Ask 4 questions per area before checking
- "More questions about [area], or move to next?"
- If more → ask 4 more, check again
- After all areas → "Ready to create context?"

**Do NOT ask about (Claude handles these):**
- Technical implementation
- Architecture choices
- Performance concerns
- Scope expansion
</process>

- Gray areas identified through intelligent analysis
- User chose which areas to discuss
- Each selected area explored until satisfied
- Scope creep redirected to deferred ideas
- CONTEXT.md captures decisions, not vague vision
- User knows next steps
</success_criteria>

{

      "header": "Discuss",

          "description": "Wave agents call LLMService directly. Should they route through SemanticAnalyzer for deeper multi-pass analysis, or enhance the existing LLM calls with richer prompts? (SemanticAnalyzer exists at semantic-analyzer.ts:119, SemanticAnalysisAgent at semantic-analysis-agent.ts:66)"
        },
        {
          "label": "Agent orchestration order",
          "description": "How should the 5 agents chain within each wave? Sequential pipeline (analyze → persist → insight → ontology), or keep current pattern where some run post-wave? (Currently: ontology runs pre-persist, insights run as Wave 4 finalization)"
        },
        {
          "label": "Insight generation scope",
          "description": "Phase 6 decided insights run as finalization after all waves. Should this change now that agents are being integrated deeper? Currently Wave 4 generates insights for all entities at once."
        },
        {
          "label": "Per-wave vs per-entity agents",
          "description": "Should each agent (semantic, persist, insight, ontology) run once per wave on all entities, or once per entity within a wave? Affects parallelism, error isolation, and resource usage."
        }
      ],
      "multiSelect": true
    }
  ]
}
```

        },
        {
          "label": "Enhance existing LLM calls",
          "description": "Keep wave agents calling LLMService directly but improve prompts with more code context, structured output requirements, and multi-observation extraction. Lighter touch, faster iteration."
        },
        {
          "label": "Hybrid approach",
          "description": "Wave 1 (project survey) uses enhanced prompts (lightweight). Wave 2+3 (component/detail) route through SemanticAnalyzer for deep code-grounded analysis. Matches depth to level."
        }
      ],
      "multiSelect": false
    }
  ]
}

      "header": "Analysis",
      "options": [

        },
        {
          "label": "Sub-step integration",
          "description": "Wave agent uses SemanticAnalyzer for code analysis + observation generation only, then constructs entities itself (adding hierarchy fields, relationships, suggested children). Wave agent stays in control."
        },
        {
          "label": "You decide",
          "description": "Claude picks the approach that best fits the existing SemanticAnalyzer interface and wave agent architecture."
        }
      ],

    }
  ]
}
